1	# Sample AEM project template
2	
3	This is a project template for AEM-based applications. It is intended as a best-practice set of examples as well as a potential starting point to develop your own functionality.
4	
5	## Modules
6	
7	The main parts of the template are:
8	
9	* core: Java bundle containing all core functionality like OSGi services, listeners or schedulers, as well as component-related Java code such as servlets or request filters.
10	* it.tests: Java based integration tests
11	* ui.apps: contains the /apps (and /etc) parts of the project, ie JS&CSS clientlibs, components, and templates
12	* ui.content: contains sample content using the components from the ui.apps
13	* ui.config: contains runmode specific OSGi configs for the project
14	* ui.frontend: an optional dedicated front-end build mechanism (Angular, React or general Webpack project)
15	* ui.tests: Selenium based UI tests
16	* all: a single content package that embeds all of the compiled modules (bundles and content packages) including any vendor dependencies
17	* analyse: this module runs analysis on the project which provides additional validation for deploying into AEMaaCS
18	
19	## How to build
20	
21	To build all the modules run in the project root directory the following command with Maven 3:
22	
23	    mvn clean install
24	
25	To build all the modules and deploy the `all` package to a local instance of AEM, run in the project root directory the following command:
26	
27	    mvn clean install -PautoInstallSinglePackage
28	
29	Or to deploy it to a publish instance, run
30	
31	    mvn clean install -PautoInstallSinglePackagePublish
32	
33	Or alternatively
34	
35	    mvn clean install -PautoInstallSinglePackage -Daem.port=4503
36	
37	Or to deploy only the bundle to the author, run
38	
39	    mvn clean install -PautoInstallBundle
40	
41	Or to deploy only a single content package, run in the sub-module directory (i.e `ui.apps`)
42	
43	    mvn clean install -PautoInstallPackage
44	
45	## Testing
46	
47	There are three levels of testing contained in the project:
48	
49	### Unit tests
50	
51	This show-cases classic unit testing of the code contained in the bundle. To
52	test, execute:
53	
54	    mvn clean test
55	
56	### Integration tests
57	
58	This allows running integration tests that exercise the capabilities of AEM via
59	HTTP calls to its API. To run the integration tests, run:
60	
61	    mvn clean verify -Plocal
62	
63	Test classes must be saved in the `src/main/java` directory (or any of its
64	subdirectories), and must be contained in files matching the pattern `*IT.java`.
65	
66	The configuration provides sensible defaults for a typical local installation of
67	AEM. If you want to point the integration tests to different AEM author and
68	publish instances, you can use the following system properties via Maven's `-D`
69	flag.
70	
71	| Property | Description | Default value |
72	| --- | --- | --- |
73	| `it.author.url` | URL of the author instance | `http://localhost:4502` |
74	| `it.author.user` | Admin user for the author instance | `admin` |
75	| `it.author.password` | Password of the admin user for the author instance | `admin` |
76	| `it.publish.url` | URL of the publish instance | `http://localhost:4503` |
77	| `it.publish.user` | Admin user for the publish instance | `admin` |
78	| `it.publish.password` | Password of the admin user for the publish instance | `admin` |
79	
80	The integration tests in this archetype use the [AEM Testing
81	Clients](https://github.com/adobe/aem-testing-clients) and showcase some
82	recommended [best
83	practices](https://github.com/adobe/aem-testing-clients/wiki/Best-practices) to
84	be put in use when writing integration tests for AEM.
85	
86	## Static Analysis
87	
88	The `analyse` module performs static analysis on the project for deploying into AEMaaCS. It is automatically
89	run when executing
90	
91	    mvn clean install
92	
93	from the project root directory. Additional information about this analysis and how to further configure it
94	can be found here https://github.com/adobe/aemanalyser-maven-plugin
95	
96	### UI tests
97	
98	They will test the UI layer of your AEM application using Selenium technology. 
99	
100	To run them locally:
101	
102	    mvn clean verify -Pui-tests-local-execution
103	
104	This default command requires:
105	* an AEM author instance available at http://localhost:4502 (with the whole project built and deployed on it, see `How to build` section above)
106	* Chrome browser installed at default location
107	
108	Check README file in `ui.tests` module for more details.
109	
110	## ClientLibs
111	
112	The frontend module is made available using an [AEM ClientLib](https://helpx.adobe.com/experience-manager/6-5/sites/developing/using/clientlibs.html). When executing the NPM build script, the app is built and the [`aem-clientlib-generator`](https://github.com/wcm-io-frontend/aem-clientlib-generator) package takes the resulting build output and transforms it into such a ClientLib.
113	
114	A ClientLib will consist of the following files and directories:
115	
116	- `css/`: CSS files which can be requested in the HTML
117	- `css.txt` (tells AEM the order and names of files in `css/` so they can be merged)
118	- `js/`: JavaScript files which can be requested in the HTML
119	- `js.txt` (tells AEM the order and names of files in `js/` so they can be merged
120	- `resources/`: Source maps, non-entrypoint code chunks (resulting from code splitting), static assets (e.g. icons), etc.
121	
122	## Maven settings
123	
124	The project comes with the auto-public repository configured. To setup the repository in your Maven settings, refer to:
125	
126	    http://helpx.adobe.com/experience-manager/kb/SetUpTheAdobeMavenRepository.html
127
